wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Onboarding AntiGravity project overview. Explain the platform's purpose as a production-grade employee onboarding solution, its core value proposition for streamlining new hire experiences, and the target audience including HR departments and organizations. Document the key features including candidate management, interactive module builders, real-time analytics, and mentorship tracking. Describe the technology stack integration and design philosophy. Include both conceptual overviews for stakeholders and technical highlights for developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend architecture documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity platform. Detail the Express server configuration, middleware setup including CORS, body parsing, and authentication middleware. Document the error handling strategy, logging mechanisms, and environment variable management. Explain the project structure, TypeScript configuration, and build process. Include code examples showing server initialization, middleware chain execution, and request/response flow. Address scalability considerations, performance optimization, and deployment architecture. Document the relationship between different server components and their roles in the overall system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrator Portal
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Administrator Portal in Onboarding AntiGravity. Detail the admin dashboard interface, user management capabilities, module administration features, analytics dashboard, and reporting system. Document the admin login process, user registration and management workflows, module creation and editing processes, progress analytics visualization, and report generation functionality. Explain the admin-specific permissions and access controls, including user role assignment, module publishing workflows, and system monitoring capabilities. Include practical examples of admin tasks such as adding new users, creating learning modules, generating progress reports, and managing system analytics. Address admin workflow optimization and best practices for system administration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend architecture documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity React application. Document the React application structure, entry point configuration, and build system setup with Vite. Explain the component hierarchy, file organization patterns, and TypeScript configuration. Detail the routing architecture with React Router DOM, protected routes implementation, and navigation patterns. Cover the application initialization process, environment configuration, and development workflow. Include examples of component composition, prop drilling prevention, and performance optimization strategies. Address code splitting, lazy loading, and bundle optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system architecture documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity platform. Describe the modular monolith architecture pattern and how it balances simplicity with future scalability. Document the high-level system flow from client requests through CDN, load balancer, to auto-scaling backend services. Explain the separation of concerns between frontend React SPA and backend Express services. Detail the technology stack choices including AWS infrastructure components (CloudFront, ALB, EC2/EKS), caching with Redis, and PostgreSQL database. Include architectural diagrams showing component relationships and data flow patterns. Address the decision-making process behind choosing modular monolith over microservices and the extraction strategy for future service decomposition.

wiki_catalogs.name: Module Creation and Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the module creation and management system. Document the complete workflow for creating, editing, and publishing learning modules including the admin module builder interface. Explain the module structure with title, description, status management (draft/published), and assessment URL integration. Detail the nested content creation process including sections, videos, documents, and embedded quizzes. Cover the module CRUD operations, status transitions, and bulk management capabilities. Include examples of module configuration, content organization strategies, and best practices for module design. Address the relationship between modules and their constituent sections, and how the system handles module publishing workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Progress Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the progress tracking system in Onboarding AntiGravity. Detail the real-time section completion tracking mechanism including the POST /api/progress/section endpoint for marking sections complete and the GET /api/progress/:userId endpoint for retrieving user progress. Explain the CandidateProgress model implementation with upsert operations for maintaining progress records. Document the progress calculation logic for module completion percentages and completion verification workflows. Include examples of progress tracking API usage, frontend integration patterns, and progress state management. Address data persistence strategies, error handling, and performance considerations for large-scale progress tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the authentication implementation section. Document the JWT-based authentication system including token generation, validation, and expiration handling. Explain the login endpoint implementation with role-based portal access control. Detail the bcrypt password hashing mechanism and security measures against brute force attacks. Cover the user lookup process, credential validation workflow, and error handling strategies. Include examples of authentication requests, token structure, and response formats. Address security considerations such as password salting, token storage, and session management patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Authentication endpoints. Document the login endpoint including request/response schemas, JWT token generation, role-based access control, and authentication flow. Detail the authentication middleware implementation, token validation, and session management. Include examples of successful login responses with JWT tokens, error responses for invalid credentials, and security considerations. Document the relationship between authentication and authorization, token refresh strategies, and logout mechanisms. Provide client-side integration examples and common authentication patterns used throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity platform. Include detailed prerequisites covering Node.js, PostgreSQL, and development environment setup. Provide step-by-step installation instructions for both backend and frontend components. Document environment variable configuration, database initialization, and initial seeding process. Include verification steps to ensure proper setup. Cover common setup issues and troubleshooting tips. Provide quick start examples for first-time users to understand basic functionality. Make content accessible to developers with varying experience levels while ensuring all technical requirements are clearly specified.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed component system documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity frontend. Document the component architecture including layout components, UI components, admin components, and reusable elements. Explain the component hierarchy, prop interfaces, and component composition patterns. Detail the sidebar navigation component, module card components, and admin section cards. Include examples of component usage, state management integration, and styling approaches. Cover component lifecycle, event handling, and communication patterns between parent and child components. Address component testing strategies, accessibility implementation, and responsive design patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database design documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity platform. Detail the Prisma schema definition covering all six entities: User, Module, Section, CandidateProgress, QuizAttempt, and Assessment. Document entity relationships, foreign key constraints, and indexes. Explain the data model design decisions, field definitions, and validation rules. Include database schema diagrams and sample data structures. Document the Prisma client usage patterns, database connection management, and migration strategies. Address data integrity, performance considerations, and seeding processes. Provide examples of common database operations and query patterns used throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend architecture documentation for the React-based client application. Describe the React 18 application structure with Vite build system and TypeScript implementation. Document the routing configuration using React Router DOM with protected routes and role-based navigation. Explain the state management architecture using Zustand for centralized state handling. Detail the component organization with layout components, UI components, and page components. Document the styling approach using Tailwind CSS and custom design system. Include the sidebar navigation implementation and component composition patterns. Address the integration between frontend components and backend API endpoints, including authentication state management and error handling strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Candidate Experience
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Candidate Experience in Onboarding AntiGravity. Document the candidate dashboard interface, module browsing and selection, content consumption workflows, assessment system, and profile settings. Explain the candidate login process, module navigation patterns, video content playback, document downloads, text content rendering, and mixed media handling. Detail the progress tracking system, quiz taking interface, completion verification, and certificate generation. Include examples of typical candidate workflows such as module enrollment, content consumption, assessment submission, and progress monitoring. Address candidate onboarding optimization, content delivery best practices, and user engagement strategies. Document the integration between frontend components and backend APIs for seamless candidate experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Delivery System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the content delivery system within the learning management platform. Detail how learning content is organized and presented to candidates, including video integration, document management, and mixed content handling. Explain the section-based content delivery model, content rendering patterns, and user interaction flows. Document supported content types including video URLs, document uploads, and text-based content. Cover the content consumption experience, progress tracking during content delivery, and how the system handles different media formats. Include examples of content configuration, embedding external resources, and managing content availability. Address content organization strategies and user engagement patterns for optimal learning outcomes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analytics Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the analytics dashboard system in Onboarding AntiGravity. Document the AdminDashboard and AdminAnalyticsPage components showing performance metrics, progress visualization, and user engagement analytics. Explain the analytics data collection mechanisms, reporting capabilities, and system health monitoring features. Detail the chart implementations using Recharts for progress visualization and performance insights. Include examples of dashboard customization, metric interpretation, and trend analysis. Address data aggregation strategies, real-time updates, and export functionality for analytics reports. Document the integration between frontend analytics components and backend data sources.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authorization and Permissions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authorization and permissions documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity platform. Detail the role-based access control (RBAC) system with ADMIN, CANDIDATE, and MENTOR roles. Document the frontend route protection mechanisms using React Router guards and Zustand store integration. Explain the backend middleware implementation for enforcing authorization rules. Cover portal-specific access control with role validation and permission enforcement. Include examples of protected routes, role-based UI rendering, and API endpoint authorization. Address common authorization scenarios, permission inheritance, and security boundary enforcement.

wiki_catalogs.name: Modules API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the Modules management endpoints. Document CRUD operations for modules including creation, retrieval, updates, and deletion. Detail the module structure with nested sections, content types (video, documents, text), and status management (draft/published). Include request/response schemas for module creation, section management, and content delivery. Document filtering, sorting, and pagination options. Provide examples of module hierarchies, content embedding, and mixed media handling. Address module permissions, access control, and content validation rules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity platform. Describe the high-level design showing the modular monolith architecture with clear separation between frontend and backend. Document component interactions between React frontend, Express backend, and PostgreSQL database. Explain the data flow from user requests through API endpoints to database operations. Detail the authentication flow using JWT tokens and role-based access control. Include system context diagrams showing how different components interact. Address the MVC pattern implementation, middleware architecture, and state management approach. Document technology stack decisions and architectural trade-offs made during development.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive state management documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity React application using Zustand. Document the centralized state store configuration, state slices, and actions for managing user authentication, progress tracking, and application state. Explain the store patterns, state mutations, and subscription mechanisms. Detail the integration with React components, state persistence strategies, and state synchronization with backend APIs. Include examples of state selectors, async state updates, and state debugging techniques. Address performance considerations, state normalization, and memory management in the frontend application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed authentication system documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity platform. Document the JWT-based authentication implementation including token generation, validation, and refresh strategies. Explain the bcrypt password hashing mechanism and security best practices. Detail the role-based access control system with ADMIN, CANDIDATE, and MENTOR roles. Document the authentication middleware, protected routes, and permission enforcement. Include code examples for login/logout flows, token management, and user registration. Address security considerations including CSRF protection, token expiration, and session management. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common authentication issues and integration patterns for frontend authentication state management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend architecture documentation for the Node.js/Express server implementation. Describe the modular route-based architecture with clear separation of concerns for authentication, modules, users, progress tracking, and analytics. Document the Express server configuration including middleware setup, CORS handling, and error management. Explain the Prisma ORM integration for database abstraction and the repository pattern implementation. Detail the authentication middleware and JWT token handling. Document the API endpoint structure following RESTful conventions and the controller-layer organization. Include the database connection management, environment configuration, and error handling strategies. Address the middleware chain and request processing pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: Mentor Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Mentor Interface in Onboarding AntiGravity. Detail the mentor dashboard, mentee management capabilities, progress supervision features, and communication tools. Document the mentor login process, mentee assignment workflows, progress monitoring interfaces, and performance analytics for assigned candidates. Explain the mentor-mentee relationship management, progress tracking visualization, and feedback mechanisms. Include examples of mentor activities such as reviewing mentee progress, providing guidance, monitoring completion rates, and generating performance reports. Address mentor training requirements, communication best practices, and effective supervision strategies. Document the integration between mentor features and the broader platform functionality for seamless mentor-mentee coordination.

wiki_catalogs.name: Assessment and Quiz System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the assessment and quiz system functionality. Document the complete quiz creation workflow including question types, answer options, and correct answer configuration. Explain the Excel import functionality for bulk quiz question creation, including the template format and validation rules. Detail the quiz execution process, question randomization, and assessment submission handling. Cover the integration between modules and embedded quizzes, assessment URL linking, and quiz completion tracking. Include examples of quiz configuration, Excel template usage, and assessment scoring mechanisms. Address quiz security considerations, timing controls, and feedback generation. Document the relationship between quiz questions and their corresponding sections within the learning modules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Users API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Users management endpoints. Document user registration, profile management, role assignment, and user administration features. Detail the user data model, authentication requirements, and permission levels (ADMIN, CANDIDATE, MENTOR). Include request/response schemas for user creation, updates, and deletion. Document user search, filtering, and bulk operations. Provide examples of user onboarding workflows, role-based access patterns, and user management dashboards. Address user data validation, password policies, and account security measures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend development documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity platform. Cover the Express server configuration, middleware setup, and error handling strategies. Document the complete API endpoint structure including authentication, module management, user administration, progress tracking, quiz system, and analytics endpoints. Explain the Prisma ORM implementation, database schema design, and data model relationships. Detail the authentication system using JWT tokens and bcrypt password hashing. Include code examples for common backend operations, API usage patterns, and integration approaches. Address security considerations, performance optimization, and database management best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing and Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed routing and navigation documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity frontend. Document the React Router DOM configuration, route definitions, and protected route implementation. Explain the navigation patterns for different user roles (admin, candidate, mentor), route guards, and permission-based access control. Detail the sidebar navigation component, breadcrumb implementation, and navigation state management. Include examples of programmatic navigation, route parameters handling, and query string management. Address navigation performance optimization, route preloading strategies, and fallback/error boundary handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all Onboarding AntiGravity backend endpoints. Document RESTful API endpoints for authentication, module management, user administration, progress tracking, quiz system, analytics, and reporting. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and error responses. Include detailed parameter descriptions, validation rules, and example requests/responses. Document the API versioning strategy, rate limiting, and pagination patterns. Address common use cases, integration patterns, and client implementation guidelines. Provide curl examples and code snippets for popular programming languages. Include troubleshooting guidance for API errors and performance optimization tips.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database design documentation for the PostgreSQL implementation using Prisma ORM. Detail the complete entity relationship model covering identity & access domain (users, roles, audit logs), core learning domain (modules, lessons, prerequisites, user_progress), assessment domain (quizzes, quiz_questions, quiz_options, user_quiz_attempts, tasks, task_submissions), and social & notification domain (mentorships, notifications). Document the UUID primary key strategy, soft delete functionality, and timestamp management. Explain the foreign key relationships and referential integrity constraints. Detail the indexing strategy for performance optimization and query patterns. Include the database seeding process and initial data setup. Address data validation rules, business logic constraints, and data lifecycle management including audit logging.

wiki_catalogs.name: Learning Progress Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the learning progress tracking system. Detail how candidate progress is monitored and recorded throughout the learning journey, including section completion tracking, module progress calculation, and overall course completion verification. Explain the progress data model, real-time progress updates, and completion verification mechanisms. Document the integration between content consumption and progress recording, including automatic progress updates and manual completion markers. Cover the progress visualization in dashboards, progress analytics, and reporting capabilities. Include examples of progress tracking configuration, completion criteria settings, and progress monitoring workflows. Address progress synchronization across different devices and content types, and how the system handles partial completions and reattempts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Progress API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the Progress tracking endpoints. Document progress monitoring, completion verification, and real-time progress updates. Detail the progress data model, candidate progress tracking, and module completion algorithms. Include request/response schemas for progress updates, completion verification, and progress reporting. Document the relationship between sections, modules, and overall progress calculation. Provide examples of progress visualization, completion thresholds, and progress analytics. wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend development documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity React application. wiki_catalogs.name: Styling and Design System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive styling and design system documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity frontend. Document the Tailwind CSS configuration, design tokens, and utility-first styling approach. Explain the component styling patterns, responsive design implementation, and dark/light theme support. Detail the custom CSS architecture, global styles, and component-specific styling strategies. Include examples of design system usage, color schemes, typography scales, and spacing systems. Address CSS-in-JS alternatives, style optimization techniques, and browser compatibility considerations. Cover accessibility compliance, responsive breakpoints, and performance impact of styling approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models and ORM
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data models and ORM documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity platform. Document all Prisma data models including User, Module, Section, CandidateProgress, QuizAttempt, and Assessment entities. Detail field definitions, data types, constraints, and relationships between models. Explain Prisma client usage patterns, query operations, and transaction handling. Document database migration strategies, schema evolution, and seeding processes. Include examples of complex queries, joins, and aggregations. Address performance optimization techniques, indexing strategies, and database best practices. Provide guidance on extending models, adding new entities, and maintaining data integrity across model changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for data flow patterns throughout the Onboarding AntiGravity platform. Describe the request-response cycle from frontend components through API endpoints to database operations. Document the authentication flow using JWT tokens and session management. Explain the progress tracking data flow from user interactions to database persistence and real-time updates. Detail the module content delivery patterns and how different content types (video, documents, text) are handled. Document the assessment submission workflow including quiz grading and task evaluation processes. Include the state synchronization patterns between frontend and backend, caching strategies, and data consistency mechanisms. Address error propagation, retry mechanisms, and data validation throughout the flow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Quiz API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Quiz and Assessment system endpoints. Document quiz creation, question management, assessment submission, and grading functionality. Detail the quiz data model, question types (multiple choice), answer validation, and scoring algorithms. Include request/response schemas for quiz creation, question import/export, and assessment responses. Document Excel import functionality, quiz scheduling, and timed assessments. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the user role-based access control system in Onboarding AntiGravity. wiki_catalogs.name: Analytics API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the Analytics and Reporting endpoints. wiki_catalogs.name: Learning Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Learning Management System within Onboarding AntiGravity. Explain the assessment system including quiz creation, question types, Excel import functionality, and grading mechanisms. wiki_catalogs.name: Reports API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Reports generation endpoints. Document report creation, export functionality, and data extraction capabilities. Detail the report data model, export formats (PDF, CSV), and report templates. Include request/response schemas for report generation, filtering options, and download mechanisms. Document report scheduling, automated reporting, and custom report creation. Provide examples of report workflows, data visualization, and export patterns. Address report security, access control, and performance optimization for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Progress Tracking and Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the progress tracking and analytics system in Onboarding AntiGravity. Document the real-time progress monitoring implementation including section completion tracking and module progress calculation. Explain the analytics dashboard features showing performance metrics, progress visualization, and user engagement analytics. Detail the data collection mechanisms for tracking candidate progress and completion verification. Cover the reporting capabilities and system health monitoring features. Include examples of progress tracking workflows, analytics interpretation, and performance insights. Address data privacy considerations and reporting customization options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security and Authentication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security and authentication documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity platform. Detail the JWT-based authentication implementation including token generation, validation, and refresh strategies. Document the password security measures using bcrypt hashing and session management. Explain the role-based access control system and route protection mechanisms. Cover security best practices implemented throughout the application including input validation, SQL injection prevention, and XSS protection. Include examples of secure API usage, authentication flow patterns, and authorization enforcement. Address common security vulnerabilities and mitigation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity project. Detail the testing requirements including unit testing, integration testing, and testing patterns used throughout the application. Cover the build configuration for both frontend and backend components. Include examples of proper code structure, error handling patterns, and performance optimization techniques. wiki_catalogs.name: Build and Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive build and deployment documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity platform. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and debugging documentation for the Onboarding AntiGravity platform. Document common issues including CORS configuration problems, database connection failures, authentication errors, and performance optimization challenges. Address error handling patterns and graceful degradation strategies. Provide escalation procedures for complex issues.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Onboarding AntiGravity Platform - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
Onboarding AntiGravity is a comprehensive employee onboarding platform designed to streamline and enhance the new hire experience through structured learning modules, interactive assessments, mentorship programs, and administrative oversight. The platform serves as a centralized solution for organizations to onboard employees efficiently while maintaining robust tracking and reporting capabilities.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Streamlined Onboarding Experience**: Provide a structured, engaging learning journey for new employees
- **Administrative Efficiency**: Enable HR and managers to oversee onboarding progress and performance
- **Assessment and Tracking**: Implement comprehensive evaluation systems including quizzes and practical tasks
- **Mentorship Integration**: Facilitate structured mentor-mentee relationships during onboarding
- **Data-Driven Insights**: Offer analytics and reporting for continuous improvement

### Target Audience
- **Administrators/HR Teams**: Manage onboarding workflows, track progress, and generate reports
- **Mentors**: Guide and support new hires throughout their onboarding journey
- **Candidates/New Employees**: Complete learning modules, assessments, and track their progress
- **Organizations**: Companies seeking to standardize and optimize their employee onboarding processes

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

The platform follows a **Modular Monolith Architecture** with clear separation of concerns across frontend and backend components:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[React Frontend] --> B[Express Backend API]
    B --> C[Prisma ORM]
    C --> D[(PostgreSQL Database)]
    
    A --> E[Authentication]
    A --> F[Learning Modules]
    A --> G[Mentorship System]
    A --> H[Analytics Dashboard]
    
    B --> I[User Management]
    B --> J[Module Management]
    B --> K[Assessment Engine]
    B --> L[Progress Tracking]
    B --> M[Report Generation]
```

### Design Patterns
- **Repository Pattern**: Implemented through Prisma ORM for data access abstraction
- **MVC Architecture**: Clear separation between Models, Views, and Controllers
- **Middleware Pattern**: Express middleware for authentication, CORS, and request processing
- **State Management**: Zustand for centralized application state management
- **Component-Based Architecture**: React components with clear props and state management

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client as React Client
    participant Auth as Authentication API
    participant Modules as Modules API
    participant Progress as Progress API
    participant DB as PostgreSQL
    
    Client->>+Auth: Login Request
    Auth->>+DB: Validate Credentials
    DB-->>-Auth: Return User Data
    Auth-->>-Client: JWT Token
    
    Client->>+Modules: Get Modules
    Modules->>+DB: Fetch Module Data
    DB-->>-Modules: Return Modules
    Modules-->>-Client: Module List
    
    Client->>+Progress: Update Progress
    Progress->>+DB: Save Progress
    DB-->>-Progress: Confirm Save
    Progress-->>-Client: Success Response
```

### Data Flow Diagrams

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Interface] --> B[React Router]
    B --> C[Zustand Store]
    C --> D[API Layer]
    D --> E[Express Server]
    E --> F[Prisma ORM]
    F --> G[(PostgreSQL)]
    
    H[Admin Panel] --> B
    I[Candidate Dashboard] --> B
    J[Mentor Interface] --> B
    
    K[Authentication] --> E
    L[Module Management] --> E
    M[Progress Tracking] --> E
    N[Analytics Reports] --> E
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Entry Point**: `backend/src/index.ts`
- Initializes Express server with CORS configuration
- Sets up route handlers for all API endpoints
- Configures development and production environments
- Implements health check endpoint for monitoring

**Frontend Entry Point**: `frontend/src/main.tsx`
- Bootstraps React application with Vite
- Configures routing and state management
- Integrates Tailwind CSS for styling
- Sets up global providers and context

### Core Modules

**Database Schema**: `backend/prisma/schema.prisma`
- Defines comprehensive data model with User, Module, Section, Document, QuizQuestion, CandidateProgress, and QuizAttempt entities
- Implements UUID primary keys for scalability
- Establishes relationships between entities with proper foreign key constraints
- Includes indexes for optimal query performance

**State Management**: `frontend/src/store/useStore.ts`
- Implements centralized state management using Zustand
- Manages user authentication state and role-based permissions
- Handles overall progress tracking across modules
- Provides localStorage persistence for user sessions

### Configuration Approach

**Backend Configuration**: `backend/tsconfig.json`
- TypeScript configuration for Node.js backend
- ES2020 target with CommonJS module system
- Strict type checking enabled
- Root directory mapping to `src`

**Frontend Configuration**: `frontend/tsconfig.app.json`
- Modern TypeScript configuration for React application
- ES2023 target with ESNext module resolution
- JSX transformation for React components
- Strict type checking and unused variable detection

**Build Configuration**: `frontend/vite.config.ts`
- Vite build system configuration
- React plugin integration
- Development and production optimizations
- Asset handling and bundling strategy

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies**:
- `@prisma/client`: Database ORM for type-safe database operations
- `express`: Web framework for REST API development
- `bcrypt`: Password hashing for secure authentication
- `jsonwebtoken`: JWT token generation and validation
- `cors`: Cross-origin resource sharing configuration
- `multer`: File upload handling for Excel imports

**Frontend Dependencies**:
- `react` & `react-dom`: Core React library
- `react-router-dom`: Client-side routing
- `zustand`: Lightweight state management
- `framer-motion`: Animation library
- `lucide-react`: Icon library
- `recharts`: Data visualization components
- `clsx` & `tailwind-merge`: Utility classes for styling

### Integration Points

**Authentication Flow**: `backend/src/routes/auth.ts`
- JWT-based authentication system
- Role-based access control (ADMIN, CANDIDATE, MENTOR)
- Password hashing with bcrypt
- Session management with token refresh

**Module Management**: `backend/src/routes/modules.ts`
- CRUD operations for learning modules
- Nested creation of sections, documents, and quiz questions
- Excel import functionality for bulk question creation
- Optimized single-module retrieval for candidate interface

**Progress Tracking**: `backend/src/routes/progress.ts`
- Candidate progress monitoring
- Completion tracking for modules and sections
- Automatic progress calculation and updates
- Integration with user assessment data

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Sidebar Component] --> B[Navigation Links]
    B --> C[Route Guards]
    C --> D[Protected Routes]
    
    E[App Component] --> F[Router Configuration]
    F --> G[Route Definitions]
    G --> H[Page Components]
    
    I[Store Provider] --> J[State Management]
    J --> K[Component State]
    K --> L[UI Updates]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multi-Roles System**: The platform supports three distinct user roles with different capabilities:
- **Admin**: Full system access, user management, module creation, analytics dashboard
- **Mentor**: Mentorship assignment, progress monitoring, communication tools
- **Candidate**: Learning module access, assessment completion, progress tracking

**Learning Management**: Comprehensive course delivery system with:
- Structured modules containing multiple sections
- Mixed content types (videos, documents, text)
- Interactive quiz system with Excel import capability
- Progress tracking and completion verification

**Assessment and Evaluation**: Robust assessment system:
- Multiple-choice questions with randomized options
- Practical task submissions with file uploads
- Automated grading with detailed feedback
- Performance analytics and reporting

### Implementation Highlights

**Real-time Progress Tracking**: `frontend/src/pages/CandidateDashboard.tsx`
- Dynamic progress bars showing completion percentages
- Visual indicators for module status (locked, in-progress, completed)
- Integrated timeline showing learning path progression

**Admin Dashboard**: `frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboard.tsx`
- Comprehensive analytics and reporting
- User enrollment management
- Module publishing workflow
- Performance metrics visualization

**Excel Integration**: `backend/src/routes/modules.ts`
- Sample quiz template generation for easy question creation
- Bulk question import from Excel spreadsheets
- Validation and error handling for data integrity

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> CandidateDashboard: Login as Candidate
    Unauthenticated --> AdminDashboard: Login as Admin
    Unauthenticated --> MentorDashboard: Login as Mentor
    
    CandidateDashboard --> ModuleViewPage: View Module
    ModuleViewPage --> QuizPage: Take Quiz
    QuizPage --> ModuleViewPage: Submit Quiz
    ModuleViewPage --> CandidateDashboard: Complete Module
    
    AdminDashboard --> AdminModulesList: Manage Modules
    AdminModulesList --> AdminModulesBuilder: Create Module
    AdminModulesBuilder --> AdminModulesList: Save Module
    AdminDashboard --> AdminUsersPage: Manage Users
    AdminDashboard --> AdminAnalyticsPage: View Analytics
    
    MentorDashboard --> MentorshipPage: Manage Mentees
    MentorshipPage --> MentorDashboard: Update Progress
```

**Performance Optimization**: The platform implements several optimization strategies:
- Database indexing for frequently queried columns
- Lazy loading for large datasets
- Efficient API endpoints with selective data fetching
- Caching strategies for static content and module data

**Security Implementation**: Multi-layered security approach:
- JWT-based authentication with token refresh
- Role-based authorization for sensitive operations
- Input validation and sanitization
- CORS configuration for cross-origin protection
- Secure password hashing with bcrypt
